# detect_ghost { #tspaint.detect_ghost }

```python
tspaint.detect_ghost(
    ts,
    labels,
    samples,
    *,
    fit_thresh=0.6,
    depth_thresh=0.9,
    K=2,
    Q0=None,
    max_iter=8,
    soft_refs=None,
)
```

Ghost-source detection: tracts from a population **not in the panel** (Plan A Workflow 3).

A ghost / archaic tract fits **no** panel source (low ``fit``) **and** sits on an
anomalously **deep** branch relative to every labelled reference (high ``depth``). That
second criterion is what separates a genuine unsampled-source tract from a merely
uninformative (50/50, shallow) one. Detection, not attribution — it flags that a tract
descends from something outside the panel, not what (CLAUDE.md §9).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Tree sequence to analyse.

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Reference sample id -> ancestry-state label.

<code>[**samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[iterable](`iterable`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Samples to scan (typically the queries).

<code>[**fit_thresh**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.6]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Flag where ``fit < fit_thresh`` (fits no panel source). Default ``0.6``.

<code>[**depth_thresh**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.9]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Flag where the rank-normalised nearest-reference ``depth >= depth_thresh`` (a deep outlier). Default ``0.9`` (the deepest 10%) — measured on an archaic-like ghost sim: recall 0.58 at precision 1.0 and a ~1% false-positive burden on the no-ghost control; lower it to ``0.8`` for recall ~1.0 at precision ~0.86 (same control floor). The right value scales with how prevalent the ghost ancestry is — tune against your own no-ghost control. The genome-wide ghost **burden** separates ghost from no-ghost ~10x even when per-tract recall is partial.

<code>[**K**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Model / EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).

<code>[**Q0**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Model / EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).

<code>[**max_iter**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Model / EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).

<code>[**soft_refs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Model / EM controls (see :func:`tspaint.fit`).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[GhostResult](`tspaint.introgression.GhostResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``.burden`` (per-sample flagged fraction) and ``.tracts(sample)`` (the merged ghost ``(left, right)`` tracts) — the same accessors as :class:`tspaint.archaic.ArchaicResult`.
